title: Robust CLI: subcommands, args, exit codes, and helpful errors
when: Building a command-line tool or utility
principle: Use a real arg parser (subcommands + typed args + --help); validate inputs and print a clear message to stderr on bad input; return meaningful exit codes (0 success, non-zero failure); keep side-effecting logic in functions so it's testable; support --version. Read from stdin/files explicitly, never assume.
exemplar: if not args.name: print('error: name required', file=sys.stderr); return 2
pitfalls: Parsing sys.argv by hand, no --help, swallowing errors, always exiting 0, mixing I/O with logic so nothing is testable.
